pattern: Classic next-permutation on the decimal digits: find the rightmost ascending pair, swap with the smallest larger digit to its right, then reverse the suffix; reject results that overflow a 32-bit signed integer.
hints: Treat the number as a sequence of digits and ask for the next lexicographically larger arrangement of those same digits.
Scan from the right for the first digit smaller than the one after it; that pivot is where a swap can increase the value minimally.
Swap the pivot with the smallest digit to its right that is still larger, then sort the suffix ascending, and finally check the value still fits in a signed 32-bit range.

edge_inputs.description: descending digits have no next arrangement

stress.note: answer is fully determined by the digit arrangement
